data.name: current_subway_schedule
data.description: **PRE-DEFINED VARIABLE (dict)**: Dictionary containing the current subway scheduling configuration for all lines with time-based scheduling support. CRITICAL: (1) Use copy.deepcopy() to copy structure - DO NOT manually reconstruct. (2) DO NOT MODIFY station_id or time_range values. (3) ONLY modify headway and dwell_time values. Use directly: `current_subway_schedule`, NOT `current_subway_schedule()`.

timetable.time_range: [start_time, end_time] in seconds (list of 2 ints). Time periods within an hour for different scheduling strategies
timetable.headway: time between trains in seconds (int). CONSTRAINTS: min=100 (1.67 min), max=500 (8.33 min), default=153 (2.55 min)

schedule.dwell_time: time spent at station in seconds (int). CONSTRAINTS: min=25, max=50, default=30

time_range.description: Time periods in seconds within simulation. Multiple time ranges allow different headways for peak/off-peak hours. Time ranges must not overlap and should cover the entire simulation period. WARNING: DO NOT MODIFY time_range values when creating new configurations - always copy them exactly from current_subway_schedule.

headway.description: Minimum 100 seconds (1.67 min) to ensure safe train separation. Maximum 500 seconds (8.33 min) to maintain reasonable service frequency.

dwell_time.description: Minimum 25 seconds for passenger boarding/alighting. Maximum 50 seconds to avoid excessive delays.

functions.name: read_subway_states
functions.description: Query historical subway operation data. Returns a DICT with keys: 'snapshots', 'metadata', 'total_snapshots'. Access snapshots via data['snapshots']. Each station in snapshot.subway.lines[line_id].stations[station_id] has 'waiting_count' (int), 'avg_waiting_time', 'max_waiting_time', 'waiting_time_distribution'. DO NOT use open() or json.load() directly. Automatically uses the most recent traffic states file.

input_format.start_time: Optional start time filter in seconds (float, default: None)
input_format.end_time: Optional end time filter in seconds (float, default: None)
input_format.exact_time: Optional exact time filter in seconds (float, default: None)
input_format.line_id: Optional filter by specific subway line (str, default: None)
input_format.max_snapshots: Optional maximum number of snapshots to return (int, default: None for all)

metadata.file_path: str - path to traffic states file

line_id.active_trains: int - number of trains currently running on this line
line_id.station_count: int - number of stations on this line

train_id.next_station_dwell_time: float - planned dwell time at next station (seconds)

station_id.waiting_count: int - number of passengers waiting at station

waiting_time_distribution.0-60s: int - count of passengers waiting 0-60 seconds
waiting_time_distribution.60-180s: int - count of passengers waiting 60-180 seconds
waiting_time_distribution.180-300s: int - count of passengers waiting 180-300 seconds
waiting_time_distribution.>300s: int - count of passengers waiting over 300 seconds

structure.total_snapshots: int - total number of snapshots returned
